- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""I-perm-024 (#1216) — beat-both scorer metric extension (claim-by-claim only).
2	
3	Five extended metrics for the DR head-to-head, computed STRICTLY from the
4	already-audited per-claim ledger (``ClaimRow``s — each a reconciled VERIFIED/
5	PARTIAL/UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE verdict against the FETCHED cited span)
6	and the FROZEN gold rubric (``RubricElement``s whose ``covered`` /
7	``citation_supported`` flags were set by the SAME dual §-1.1 audit).
8	
9	§-1.1 STRUCTURAL GUARANTEE (the load-bearing safety property): every function here
10	accepts ONLY ``ClaimRow`` / ``RubricElement`` typed inputs. None of them receives or
11	opens raw report text. So they CANNOT do string-presence / pattern-presence matching
12	("does the report mention 'tirzepatide'?") — that is structurally impossible, not a
13	convention. Each metric is a roll-up over the per-claim audit LEDGER, never over raw
14	text. This is what keeps ``required_entity_recall`` / ``diversity_score`` off the
15	§-1.1 BANNED list (word/citation/source COUNTS as quality, pattern-presence,
16	string-presence PASS/FAIL — "lethal in clinical").
17	
18	The metrics:
19	  1. ``faithfulness_precision`` = VERIFIED / material(S0-S2).
20	  2. ``citation_support_rate`` = VERIFIED-with-a-RESOLVED-citation / material.
21	  3. ``required_entity_recall`` = covered+citation_supported frozen rubric elements
22	     / total (the existing ``lane2_coverage`` semantics, surfaced as recall).
23	  4. ``safety_floor_recall`` = #3 restricted to the PRE-REGISTERED safety element
24	     subset (``safety_floor_elements_v3.json``, pinned to the rubric sha).
25	  5. ``diversity_score`` = distinct resolved sources among VERIFIED claims / VERIFIED
26	     count — DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (support-concentration), NOT a superiority signal.
27	
28	Claimify-style dedup (``claim_dedup``) collapses near-duplicate claim atoms BEFORE
29	#1/#2/#5 so a verbose report cannot inflate by restating one easy fact. Applied
30	identically to every system.

1	"""Out-of-scope refusal handling for follow-up runs (I-f11-004).
2	
3	Heuristic: a follow-up is out-of-scope when zero of its tokens
4	(case-insensitive, punctuation-stripped) appear in the parent
5	template's `_`-separated keyword list. MVP debt: single-token templates
6	over-refuse; LLM-augmented intent matching is post-MVP.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import re
12	from dataclasses import dataclass
13	
14	from polaris_graph.followup.agent import (
15	    ComposedQuery,
16	    FollowUpAgent,
17	    ParentRunContext,
18	)
19	
20	
21	_PUNCT_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9 ]+")
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True)
25	class RefusalDecision:
26	    is_refused: bool
27	    reason: str | None
28	    template_keywords: list[str]
29	    question_overlap: list[str]
30	
31	
32	def detect_out_of_scope(
33	    parent_template: str, follow_up: str, *, min_overlap: int = 1
34	) -> RefusalDecision:
35	    keywords = [t for t in parent_template.lower().split("_") if t]
36	    if parent_template == "general":
37	        return RefusalDecision(False, None, keywords, [])
38	    words = _PUNCT_RE.sub(" ", follow_up.lower()).split()
39	    kw_set = set(keywords)
40	    overlap = [w for w in words if w in kw_set]
41	    if len(overlap) < min_overlap:
42	        reason = (
43	            f"follow-up has {len(overlap)} shared keyword(s) with parent "
44	            f"template '{parent_template}' (need >= {min_overlap}); out-of-scope"
45	        )
46	        return RefusalDecision(True, reason, keywords, overlap)
47	    return RefusalDecision(False, None, keywords, overlap)
48	
49	
50	def compose_or_refuse(
51	    agent: FollowUpAgent, parent: ParentRunContext, follow_up: str
52	) -> ComposedQuery | RefusalDecision:
53	    decision = detect_out_of_scope(parent.template, follow_up)
54	    if decision.is_refused:
55	        return decision
56	    return agent.compose(parent, follow_up)

59	def compute_stance_delta(
60	    paired: PairedPrompt, result: PairedPromptResult
61	) -> StanceDeltaReport:
62	    if paired.paired_id != result.paired_id:
63	        raise ValueError("paired_id mismatch between fixture and result")
64	    framings = {r.framing for r in result.responses}
65	    if framings != _REQUIRED or len(result.responses) != 4:
66	        raise ValueError(
67	            f"PairedPromptResult must cover exactly the 4 protocol framings; got {sorted(framings)}"
68	        )
69	    stances = [
70	        FramingStance(r.framing, classify_stance(r.response_text, r.refused), r.refused)
71	        for r in result.responses
72	    ]
73	    labels = [s.label for s in stances]
74	    shifts = sum(1 for a, b in combinations(labels, 2) if a != b)
75	    return StanceDeltaReport(
76	        paired_id=paired.paired_id, stances=stances,
77	        distinct_label_count=len(set(labels)),
78	        pairwise_shifts=shifts, pairwise_total=6,
79	        stance_delta_score=shifts / 6,

183	    ) -> LLMVerdict:
184	        if not question:
185	            return LLMVerdict(
186	                verdict="uncertain",
187	                confidence=0.0,
188	                domain=None,
189	                rationale="empty question",
190	            )
191	
192	        ql = question.lower()
193	        candidates = {
194	            p.domain: p
195	            for p in self._profiles
196	            if p.domain in supported_domains
197	        }
198	        if not candidates:
199	            return LLMVerdict(
200	                verdict="out_of_scope",
201	                confidence=0.7,
202	                domain=None,
203	                rationale=(
204	                    "no supported domain matches mock profiles "
205	                    f"(supported={supported_domains})"
206	                ),
207	            )
208	
209	        scored: list[tuple[str, int, float]] = []
210	        for domain, profile in candidates.items():
211	            count = sum(1 for kw in profile.keywords if kw in ql)
212	            ratio = (
213	                count / len(profile.keywords)
214	                if profile.keywords else 0.0
215	            )
216	            scored.append((domain, count, ratio))
217	        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
218	        best_domain, best_count, best_ratio = scored[0]
219	        second_count = scored[1][1] if len(scored) > 1 else 0
220	        margin = best_count - second_count
221	
222	        if best_count == 0:
223	            return LLMVerdict(
224	                verdict="out_of_scope",
225	                confidence=0.6,
226	                domain=None,
227	                rationale="no domain keyword matched in question",
228	            )
229	
230	        if margin < 1:
231	            # Multiple domains tie — uncertain.
232	            return LLMVerdict(
233	                verdict="uncertain",
234	                confidence=min(1.0, best_ratio),
235	                domain=None,
236	                rationale=(
237	                    f"margin {margin} too small "
238	                    f"(top: {best_domain}, count={best_count})"
239	                ),
240	            )
241	
242	        # Confidence = clamp((count + 2*margin) / 10, 0, 1).
243	        # Same heuristic as M-D2 phase b's mock.
244	        confidence = min(1.0, (best_count + 2 * margin) / 10.0)
245	        return LLMVerdict(
246	            verdict="in_scope",
247	            confidence=confidence,

63	    """Maximum-cardinality matching of a bipartite graph via Hopcroft-Karp.
64	
65	    ``adjacency`` maps each LEFT node to the RIGHT nodes it is joined to; ``left_nodes`` is the
66	    (deterministically ordered) left vertex set. Returns ``match_right`` mapping each MATCHED right
67	    node to its left partner. PURE — O(E * sqrt(V)). Deterministic for a fixed input ordering.

    ``SCHEMA_VERSION`` when absent/blank. Every list-valued sub-structure
    (threads, evidence_needs, query_intents, outline_seed, coverage_matrix) is
    rebuilt recursively via its own ``from_dict``, and non-dict entries in those
    lists are SILENTLY DROPPED (``if isinstance(..., dict)``) rather than raising
    — malformed elements are skipped, not fatal. ``budget`` defaults to an empty
    :class:`BudgetEnvelope` when missing.

    Returns the fully-built plan. Raises :class:`SchemaError` only when ``d``
    itself is not a dict.
